channel.title: Jacob's Daily Briefing
channel.description: A personal daily morning briefing covering world events, tech/AI news, calendar, messages, and local updates. Generated by Claude.
channel.itunes:author: Jacob Brooke

item.title: Week in Review — March 28, 2026
item.description:
Weekly briefing covering the week of March 22–28, 2026. Anthropic wins its landmark court case, Iran war escalates with Houthi entry, DHS shutdown worsens, and the week ahead is loaded.
00:00:00 Opening — The Week That Was 00:02:00 Highlights and Wins 00:03:15 Stories That Evolved 00:07:30 Vault Activity 00:08:30 Relationship Check 00:09:45 The Week Ahead 00:11:00 What to Watch 00:12:00 Closing
item.content:encoded:
The Week That Was

This was Anthropic's week — a 43-page federal injunction, a leaked next-gen model (Claude Mythos), iOS 27 Siri integration, and $19B ARR. Meanwhile, the Iran war escalated: Houthis entered the conflict, oil hit $112.57, and Congress left for recess while 61,000 TSA workers remain unpaid.

Anthropic v. Pentagon

Judge Rita Lin issued a preliminary injunction calling the Pentagon's designation "classic illegal First Amendment retaliation" and "Orwellian." Federal agencies can resume using Claude. The government has until April 2 to appeal. CNBC coverage →

Iran War — Days 23–29

The week opened with a 48-hour ultimatum and ended with Houthis firing on Israel. Oil surged from ~$102 to $112.57. Goldman Sachs says prices could stay elevated through 2027. The IEA called it worse than the 1970s oil shocks. Axios: Vance takes lead →

DHS Shutdown — Days 40–43

Senate passed bipartisan funding at 2:20 AM Friday. House rejected it, passed their own bill, then both chambers left for two-week recess. No resolution before mid-April.

Apple

iOS 26.4 dropped with Playlist Playground and new emoji. AirPods Max 2 pre-orders opened. WWDC confirmed June 8. Bloomberg: iOS 27 will open Siri to Claude, Gemini via Extensions system. Apple turns 50 on Wednesday.

The Week Ahead

Ben arrives Monday for carpet install Tuesday. Karen's retirement announcement Wednesday (Q2 starts). Anthropic pilot discussion Thursday morning — same day as the government's appeal deadline and dinner with friends.

item.title: Saturday Afternoon Roundup — March 28, 2026
item.description: Quick Saturday roundup: Claude subscriptions skyrocketing, Mac Pro discontinued, SoftBank's $40B OpenAI signal, YC Demo Day highlights, and Whoop's health monitoring pivot.
item.content:encoded:
Claude's Paying Users Have More Than Doubled

Anthropic reports explosive consumer growth in 2026, driven by Claude Code and smart marketing. Read more →

Apple Discontinues the Mac Pro

The Mac Pro has been pulled from Apple's website with no replacement announced. The Mac Studio effectively ate its market. Read more →

SoftBank's $40B Loan Signals OpenAI IPO

A 12-month unsecured loan to fund OpenAI investment suggests lenders expect a public offering within the year. Read more →

YC Demo Day Highlights

From moon hotels to cattle herding — the Winter 2026 batch covered a wild range. Read more →

Whoop Pivots to Health Monitoring

The wearable company wants to move beyond elite athletes to become a life-saving health monitor for everyone. Read more →

item.title: Deep Dive — Claude Mythos: Anthropic's Biggest Leak and IPO Plans
item.description: A deep dive into the Claude Mythos leak — Anthropic's new Capybara-tier model with unprecedented cybersecurity capabilities, the Wall Street selloff it triggered, and the October 2026 IPO plans revealed the same day.
item.content:encoded:
The Leak

On March 26, 2026, a CMS misconfiguration at Anthropic exposed roughly 3,000 unpublished blog assets — including a draft post describing Claude Mythos, a new model tier above Opus. Security researchers Roy Paz (LayerX) and Alexandre Pauwels (Cambridge) found the exposed data store. Fortune broke the story.

What Is Capybara?

Capybara is Anthropic's new fourth model tier, positioned above Opus. Claude Mythos is the first model in this tier. Anthropic calls it "a step change" and "the most capable we've built to date," with dramatically higher scores in coding, reasoning, and cybersecurity.

Cybersecurity Concerns

The draft warns Mythos is "far ahead of any other AI model in cyber capabilities" — able to find and exploit software vulnerabilities at machine speed. This triggered a major cybersecurity stock selloff: CrowdStrike -7%, Palo Alto Networks -6%, Tenable -11%, Zscaler -4.5%.

IPO Plans

Bloomberg reported the same day that Anthropic is targeting an October 2026 IPO, potentially raising 0B+. The company is valued at ~80B after a 0B February funding round, running at 4B annualized revenue. The timing has fueled skepticism about whether the leak was truly accidental.

Open Questions

When will Mythos be available via API? What will pricing look like? Will cybersecurity concerns trigger regulation? And was this leak intentional pre-IPO marketing?

item.title: Apple TV+ 2026: The Complete Lineup
item.description: A deep dive into everything coming to Apple TV+ in 2026 — returning favorites like Ted Lasso, Silo, and Slow Horses, plus new star-studded originals from Cape Fear to Lucky.
item.content:encoded:
Returning Series

Already premiered: Hijack S2, Drops of God S2, Tehran S3, Shrinking S3, The Last Thing He Told Me S2, Monarch: Legacy of Monsters S2

Spring/Summer: For All Mankind S5 (Mar 27), Your Friends & Neighbors S2 (Apr 3), Criminal Record S2 (Apr 22), Sugar S2 (Jun 19), Ted Lasso S4 (Summer), Silo S3 (Summer)

Fall: Slow Horses S6, The Morning Show S5

New Original Series

Eternity (Feb 13) — Elizabeth Olsen, Miles Teller. Afterlife romantic comedy. Read more →

Imperfect Women (Mar 18) — Elisabeth Moss, Kerry Washington, Kate Mara. Psychological thriller. Read more →

Margo's Got Money Troubles (Apr 15) — Elle Fanning, Michelle Pfeiffer, Nicole Kidman. David E. Kelley comedy-drama. Read more →

Widow's Bay (Apr 29) — Matthew Rhys. Horror-comedy. Read more →

Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed (May 20) — Tatiana Maslany. Dark comedy thriller. Read more →

Cape Fear (Jun 5) — Javier Bardem, Amy Adams. Scorsese reimagining. Read more →

Lucky (Jul 15) — Anya Taylor-Joy, Annette Bening, Timothy Olyphant. Limited series. Read more →

Notable Films

Outcome (Apr 10) — Keanu Reeves, Cameron Diaz, Martin Scorsese

The Dink (Jul 24) — Pickleball comedy with Ben Stiller

Mayday (Sep 4) — Ryan Reynolds, Kenneth Branagh

Matchbox The Movie (Oct 9) — John Cena

Way of the Warrior Kid (Nov 20) — Chris Pratt

What's NOT Coming in 2026

Severance S3 — Filming starts spring 2026, likely mid-2027 premiere

Bad Sisters S3 — Not renewed; creator says S2 was a natural endpoint

item.title: Daily Briefing — Saturday Evening Update, March 28, 2026
item.description: Trump extends Hormuz deadline 10 days. Dubai crude hits $126. Iranian Red Crescent reports 1,900 killed. TSA paychecks may arrive Monday. Bank of America settles Epstein lawsuit for $72.5M. Anthropic nears $19B annualized revenue. Apple partners with Google Gemini for Siri overhaul.
item.content:encoded:
Iran War — Evening Update

Trump extended the Strait of Hormuz deadline by 10 days — Iran now has until ~April 7 to reopen the strait. Dubai crude benchmark at $126/barrel, up 76% since the war started. Pakistan now acting as intermediary. Iranian Red Crescent: 1,900 killed since Feb 28. In Lebanon, 1,142 killed including 122 children.

DHS Shutdown

TSA workers should start seeing paychecks as early as Monday, March 30.

Financial

Bank of America agreed to pay $72.5M to settle Epstein-related lawsuit. Anthropic approaching $19B annualized revenue.

Apple

Apple partnering with Google to use 1.2T parameter Gemini model for reimagined Siri in iOS 26.4.

item.title: Daily Briefing — Saturday Mid-Morning Update, March 28, 2026
item.description: Houthis enter Iran war. 3,100+ No Kings protests planned. Warhorse Studios fires translator for AI. Apple turns 50 Wednesday. Cherry blossoms in Tacoma.
item.content:encoded:
Weather

Jefferson City: frost at dawn (31°F), climbing to 83°F with full sun. Tacoma: 56°F, partly cloudy, rain tomorrow.

Texts

Katelyn asking about bird sightings — you two have been trading all week. Alex sent The Verge's Apple 50th anniversary product ranking at 11 PM Friday.

Iran War Escalation

Houthis launched their first military operation in support of Iran — a ballistic missile at Israel. Debris caused fires at a UAE industrial hub. Drone hit Kuwait airport radar. The 10-day Hormuz extension is the framework but the situation is more fragile.

No Kings Protests

3,100+ demonstrations planned nationwide, organizers expecting millions. Two-thirds of RSVPs from outside major cities, up 40% from June 2025.

DHS Shutdown Day 43

Congress left for two-week recess without a deal. 61,000 TSA workers have missed three paychecks.

AI Replacement Watch

Warhorse Studios (Kingdom Come Deliverance 2) fired their Czech-to-English translator and replaced him with AI. No warning. 12K upvotes, 1,500 comments on Reddit.

Apple

Turns 50 Wednesday. iPhone Air C1X modem matches Qualcomm on speed, wins on latency. 15+ new products expected this year. No iPhone in Lockdown Mode has ever been hacked.

From Your Inbox

Claude Team: Claude Code can now keep working while you're away. 404 Media: Earth's magnetic field creating cosmic ray void. The Atlantic: America sliding toward illiteracy.

Tacoma

Cherry blossoms blooming at Wapato Hills — gorgeous photos on r/Tacoma.

item.title: Daily Briefing — Saturday, March 28, 2026
item.description: Houthis enter the Iran war with missile strikes on Israel. Ten US service members injured at Prince Sultan Air Base. Brent crude hits $112.57. DHS shutdown day 43 — Congress leaves on recess. Anthropic appeal deadline Thursday. WA bans non-competes. Apple turns 50 Wednesday. Your week ahead.
item.content:encoded:
Iran War — Day 29

The Houthis entered the war, launching ballistic missiles at Israel — their first strike since the Hamas ceasefire. At least 10 US service members were injured in an Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base. Brent crude hit $112.57, up 4.2%. Reddit surfaced a WSJ report that the US may deploy up to 17,000 troops near Iran. The UK’s Starmer reaffirmed Britain will not join.

DHS Shutdown — Day 43

The Senate passed a bipartisan funding bill, then the House rejected it and passed their own (213-203), which Senate Democrats called dead on arrival. Both chambers left for a two-week recess. 61,000+ TSA workers have missed three paychecks.

Anthropic v. Pentagon

Judge Lin’s preliminary injunction stands. Government appeal deadline is Thursday, April 2 — the same day as Jacob’s Anthropic pilot discussion at work.

Tech & AI

Semafor: A 19-year-old’s AI agent team spent £1,426 on unauthorized catering. Stratechery: RIP Sora, OpenAI killed it for GPUs. Kash Patel’s email was hacked. SoftBank taking a $40B loan for OpenAI (IPO signal). WA bans non-compete agreements.

Apple

Apple turns 50 on Wednesday. No iPhone in Lockdown Mode has ever been hacked. The C1X modem matches Qualcomm on speed.

Calendar

Monday: Ben arrives. Tuesday: Payday + carpet install. Wednesday: Q2 starts, Karen retirement announcement, Apple 50th. Thursday: Anthropic pilot 9AM, dinner with friends 6PM, government appeal deadline.

From Reddit

r/Tacoma: Cherry blossoms at Wapato Hills, No Kings protest today. r/SeattleWA: WA bans non-competes, Seattle police starting pay ~$118K. r/apple: Lockdown Mode undefeated, Apple hiring Google VP for AI.
